'''David Joseph Solomona'''<ref name="SOLOMONA, DAVID JOSEPH 2001 - 2002, 2005 - 2006 - KIWI #693">{{cite web|url=http://nzleague.co.nz/kiwis/player_profile.php?letter=S&id=693|title=SOLOMONA, DAVID JOSEPH 2001 – 2002, 2005 – 2006 – KIWI #693|publisher=nzleague.co.nz|date=31 December 2017|access-date=1 January 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120402171653/http://nzleague.co.nz/kiwis/player_profile.php?letter=S&id=693|archive-date=2 April 2012|url-status=dead}}</ref> (born 26 January 1978) is a former professional [[rugby league]] footballer, who last played for the [[Warrington Wolves]] in the [[Super League]].<ref name="Profile at loverugbyleague.com"/><ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/> He was regularly amongst the top off-loaders in the competition.

==Background==
Solomona was born in [[Auckland, New Zealand]] and is of Samoan and Egyptian descent. He attended [[St. Paul's College, Auckland]], a school known for consistently producing the best rugby league team in the national secondary school competition.

==National Rugby League==
Solomona started his career in Australia with the [[Sydney Roosters|Sydney City Roosters]] making his first grade debut in round 16 of the [[1999 NRL season]] against [[Melbourne Storm|Melbourne]]. He played from the interchange bench for the Roosters in their [[2000 NRL grand final]] loss to the [[Brisbane Broncos]]. He played for [[Samoa national rugby league team|Samoa]] in the [[2000 Rugby League World Cup]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.roosters.com.au/about/past-players/|title=Men's Honour Roll|website=Sydney Roosters|access-date=23 January 2019|archive-date=27 March 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190327090103/https://www.roosters.com.au/about/past-players/|url-status=dead}}</ref>

In 2001, Solomona signed for Parramatta.  He played 25 games for the club in the [[2001 NRL season]] as the club were runaway [[Minor Premiers]] and set a number of point scoring records.  Solomona played for the [[Parramatta Eels]] from the interchange bench in their shock [[2001 NRL Grand Final]] loss to the [[Newcastle Knights]] at [[Stadium Australia]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.parraeels.com.au/about/official-player-numbers/|title=Official Player Numbers|website=Parramatta Eels}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2017/08/11/knights-draw-2001-grand-final-inspiration/|title=Knights draw 2001 grand final inspiration|website=NRL.com|date=10 August 2017 }}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/parramatta-eels-five-times-they-should-have-been-set-for-a-big-season-but-flopped/news-story/70bdcb6a002101a0640921d067eb4c49/|title=Parramatta Eels: Five times they should have been set for a big season but flopped|website=Fox Sports|date=20 March 2018 }}</ref>

==Super League==
Solomona spent three years at [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]] where his skills helped keep them in the [[Super League]]. In 2004 he was named in the [[Super League Dream Team]]. He helped [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand]] to defeat Australia to win the [[2005 Rugby League Tri Nations]] tournament.

Solomona signed for the [[Bradford Bulls]] on 24 October 2006 for the 2007 season. He was the Bulls only ever-present player in 2007, making 32 appearances in his first year at the club. Blessed with tremendous ball handling skills he also has the ability to score tries himself and he scored 14 tries in 2007, including a hat trick.

Solomona missed the first 5 games of 2008 due to injury picked up in pre-season. He again represented Samoa in the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup]].<ref name="Samoa, Tonga and Fiji name squads">{{cite web|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/7658506.stm|title=Samoa, Tonga and Fiji name squads|publisher=BBC|date=8 October 2008|access-date=9 October 2008}}</ref>

Solomona played in the [[2010 Challenge Cup Final]] victory over the [[Leeds Rhinos]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref name="Leeds 6-30 Warrington">{{cite news|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/8938067.stm|title=Leeds 6–30 Warrington|publisher=BBC|date=28 August 2010|access-date=22 November 2019|first=Ged|last=Scott}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2010/aug/28/leeds-rhinos-warrington-wolves-challenge-cup-final |location=London |work=The Guardian |title=Chris Hicks hat-trick leads Warrington to Challenge Cup triumph over Leeds |first=Andy |last=Wilson |date=28 August 2010 |access-date=22 November 2019<!--DASHBot--> |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100831105934/http://www.guardian.co.uk/sport/2010/aug/28/leeds-rhinos-warrington-wolves-challenge-cup-final |archive-date=31 August 2010 |url-status=live |df=dmy }}</ref>

Solomona continued to feature in the [[Warrington Wolves]] squad throughout the 2011 season. He was mainly brought on as a replacement making good use of his strength and ball handling skills to make in-roads into the opposing defence.
His tenure with the Warrington Wolves came to a close due to an injury he suffered in the 2012 season, causing him to retire before returning to Australia.
